meta.title: Dawny — A task app that deletes yesterday's tasks. On purpose.
meta.description: No overdue. No carryover. No guilt. Dawny resets your day at 3 AM so you can start fresh, every single day.

headline: A task app that deletes
yesterday's tasks.
On purpose.
sub: No more abandoned tasks dragging you down.
Dawny sweeps away the digital baggage of yesterday, helping you focus on what matters now.

manifesto.quote: Overdue tasks are a design flaw.
manifesto.bodyMain: Most apps mark unfinished tasks in red — as if you failed. But your list isn't just full of things you didn't finish. It's packed with things that stopped mattering weeks ago, sitting there because deleting them feels like giving up. Dawny reads both as information, not failure.
manifesto.bodyMid: A list that never shrinks isn't a productivity tool — it's a weight. When everything stays forever, the things that matter today drown in the things that mattered last month.
manifesto.bodyAside: A red priority flag on a task doesn't automatically make it your priority. You already know, quite clearly, what matters and what doesn't.

reset.title: While you sleep,
reset.body: At 3 AM, Dawny quietly clears your Daily Focus. Nothing is lost. Unfinished tasks return safely to the Backlog. You wake up to a clean slate.

twoLists.title: Two lists. That's it.
twoLists.backlogText: Everything that matters to you — No exact dates, no detailed priorities or planning.
twoLists.focusText: What actually matters today — You decide what's on your plate today.
twoLists.progressText: Not enough time in your day? — What's left goes back to the backlog.
twoLists.captionProgress: Daily Focus — open and completed tasks

contrast.title: Other apps keep you chasing old plans.
contrast.titleAccent: Dawny gives you a fresh focus for each day.

audience.title: This will feel right if…
items: Your task list turned into a graveyard.
You reschedule more than you decide.
You want clarity, not control systems.
You think in days, not systems.
audience.footnote: Dawny tends to work especially well for people who don't fit rigid productivity systems — including many neurodivergent thinkers.

makeItCount.title: If a task has been on your list for two weeks —
makeItCount.narrative: An unchecked task isn't a failure. It's information. Choosing a task for Today is a commitment to yourself. When that commitment keeps breaking, Dawny takes the signal seriously.
makeItCount.logic: If you don't get to a task you chose for Today, it goes back to your Backlog. If it keeps slipping, Dawny quietly moves it to the Archive — your digital baggage, out of the way.
makeItCount.safety: Nothing in the Archive is gone. Restore any task to your Backlog or back to Today with a single tap.

finalCta.body: Dawny is currently in TestFlight. Your feedback directly shapes the product.
finalCta.qrAlt: Scan to open TestFlight on iOS

footer.tagline: A task app that deletes yesterday's tasks. On purpose.
footer.rights: © {year} Florian Schneider. Made with care in Germany.
